Now, real quick, I just want to focus on one last thing in terms of tonality here. I've covered this in great detail in the tonality module, but every question you ask as you qualify, each one has its own specific tonality, and you have to practice this in advance. For example, when you ask someone, how long are you thinking about becoming a trader? Let's say you also want a trading system, right? So how long are you thinking about becoming a trader? Or so tell me, how long are you thinking about becoming a trader? Not how long are you thinking about becoming a trader, or how long have you been thinking about becoming a trader? So tell me, how long have you been thinking about becoming a trader? Notice the tonality says, I really want to know. It's like I'm interested in what you're saying. So again, that the tonality allows you to really chip away at the emotional part of the message. Chip away at any core beliefs they might have, and it'll really allow you to be perceived for the caring person that you truly are. And in particular, be sure to ask your more invasive questions in a more of a disarming or almost an apologetic tone, depending on what it is. So if I was going to say this, how much money do you have in your stock account? I wouldn't say so. How much money do you have in your stock account? I'd say, so. How much money do you have in your stock account? Just a ballpark. No big deal. Just a ballpark. So I would always use that disarming tone. Well, let's say I had a product I was selling and it was someone who was leading their mortgage or their credit cards. I was helping them get current. I wouldn't say, so tell me, how late are you in your mortgage right now? Huh? Four. So what do you own your credit cards? Deadbeat. You wouldn't use that sort of aggressive tone. I would say, so tell me. So how late are you in your mortgage right now? How many? Three, four months? Just a ballpark. And how far behind? How about the credit cards? How much of a problem are those? You see, the words don't even matter here. It's the tonality that saves the day. It says, I care. I'm real. I'm not like the other people who've called you. I'm special. I'm a person that really has your best interest at heart.
